text:
ITVbosses are giving soap fans an extra special treat as its soaps go up against coverage of theOlympicsin the TV schedules.

SoEmmerdaleandCoronation Streetviewers don’t have to miss out on all the sporting action unfolding inParisover on theBBC, episodes of both soaps are dropping early on ITVX over the next two weeks.

On each day of transmission, fans will be able tostream new episodes of Corrie and Emmerdale from 7amon the platform.

To view this video please enable JavaScript, and consider upgrading to a web
							browser thatsupports HTML5
								video

Up Next

A post on the soaps’ official Instagram pages informed fans of the schedule shake-up – but they were also issued with a strict warning.

‘We’re giving you a little treat this summer whilst the Olympics are on,’ the post read.

‘But remember to keep this a spoiler free zone for those watching in the evening.’

A post shared by Coronation Street (@coronationstreet)

Both shows will still air in their regular slots on ITV1, with Emmerdale going out weeknights at 7.30pm, and Corrie following at 8pm on Monday, Wednesday and Friday.

Soap bosses similarly gave fans the chance to watch the soaps first on ITVXduring the Euros earlier this summer, as both shows were frequently moved around the schedules to accommodate coverage of the matches.

Meanwhile,EastEnders fans are facing continued disruption to their regular viewing, as the soap has been bumped out of its BBC One slot, instead airing on BBC Two at 7.30pm from Monday to Thursday during the duration of the Games.

As ever, though, each episode will be available to stream on BBC iPlayer from 6am each day.

If you’ve got a soap or TV story, video or pictures get in touch by emailing ussoaps@metro.co.uk– we’d love to hear from you.

title: Coronation Street and Emmerdale in Olympics schedule shake-up | Soaps | Metro News
description: There are changes to the ITV soap schedules for Emmerdale and Coronation Street, as coverage of the Paris 2024 Olympics continues on the BBC
